fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to tennessee, pharmacy technician compact tennessee, tennessee pharmacy technician endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Tennessee.

steps.title: Meet the Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Applicants must be at least seventeen (17) years of age and have earned a high school diploma or its equivalent.

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Apply for license as a Pharmacy Technician with the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pay the Required Fee
steps.description: Submit the required license fee of $100 to the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Tennessee. To work in Tennessee, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Tennessee
hero.dek: Tennessee Pharmacy Technician licensure requires completing an application, paying a fee, and meeting specific eligibility criteria.
